item.description: President Trump thanked President Xi during remarks on the second day of the U.S.-China summit. He said a lot of problems were settled during talks and indicated that the two leaders share similar feelings about wanting the war with Iran to end.

media:content.media:title: Trump praises Xi, says ‘wonderful things’ done during China summit
media:content.media:description: President Trump thanked President Xi during remarks on the second day of the U.S.-China summit. He said a lot of problems were settled during talks and indicated that the two leaders share similar feelings about wanting the war with Iran to end.

item.title: Trump praises Xi, says ‘wonderful things’ done during China summit

item.description: In the latest round of panda diplomacy playing out between the U.S. and China, NBC News’ Janis Mackey Frayer got exclusive access to the two pandas getting ready for their upcoming journey to Zoo Atlanta in America.

media:content.media:title: Exclusive look at Chinese pandas preparing for trip to America
media:content.media:description: In the latest round of panda diplomacy playing out between the U.S. and China, NBC News’ Janis Mackey Frayer got exclusive access to the two pandas getting ready for their upcoming journey to Zoo Atlanta in America.

item.title: Exclusive look at Chinese pandas preparing for trip to America

item.dc:creator: Abigail Williams
item.description: After living without their loved ones for more than a decade, two families of Americans detained in China are appealing to President Donald Trump to bring them home as he meets with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing.

media:content.media:text: Cynthia and Nelson Wells, Sr. with a photo of Nelson, Sr. and their son Nelson Wells, Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014, in their home in Haughton, La.
media:content.media:description: Cynthia and Nelson Wells Sr. with a photo of their son, Nelson Wells Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014.

media:content.media:text: Nelson Wells Jr.
media:content.media:description: Cynthia and Nelson Wells Sr. with a photo of their son, Nelson Wells Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014.

media:content.media:text: Dawn Michelle Hunt with her mother and older brother in Chicago in 1991.
media:content.media:description: Cynthia and Nelson Wells Sr. with a photo of their son, Nelson Wells Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014.

item.title: Families of Americans imprisoned in China appeal to Trump for help

item.dc:creator: Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: President Donald Trump’s Medicaid cuts and emerging attacks on paid family caregiving have people fearing financial ruin as they fight to keep loved ones out of institutions.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce sits on a bench on a porch next to Jason, she has her hand on his chin as he holds an orange toy
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Jason stands on a set of stairs inside of his home
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A four image composite: top left image shows a pill being poured out of a pill bottle onto a hand, top right shows Melissa helping Jason put on a shirt, bottom left show Melissa preparing food on a countertop, and bottom right shows Melissa shaving Jason's face
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce wheels her son Jason, in a wheelchair, on a sidewalk outside in a residential area
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce's hands are seen holding one of Jason's hands, there is a bump on the knuckle of his middle finger
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A large crowd of people hold signs against medicaid cuts outside
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A hand holds up a cellphone displaying a photo of Jason Gonce seated in a baby chair as a baby
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A three image composite: the image on the left shows two black t-shirt's for Casa Mia's restaurant hanging on a hanger on a door, middle image shows Melissa embracing Jason, right image shows bills and coupons on a countertop
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Michele Gregory and her partner and son (in a wheelchair) pose for a family portrait outside
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Casey Barrett and his son hug his daughter, center, who is seated in a wheelchair, she holds two teddy bears, they are all in a field
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Anna Keyzer and family members pose by a tree on a sidewalk for a family portrait
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce shaves Jason's chin in their bathroom
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Jason, left, playing with a blue toy, sits next to his brother,
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce seated at her kitchen island
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce and her two sons walk down the stairs, seen from their backs
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

item.title: Families caring for disabled relatives face unthinkable choices as Medicaid cuts loom

item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe traveled to Cuba to "personally deliver President Trump's message" to Cuban leaders that the United States is ready to engage in discussions on economic and security issues.

media:content.media:title: CIA director travels to Havana to meet with Cuban officials
media:content.media:description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe traveled to Cuba to "personally deliver President Trump's message" to Cuban leaders that the United States is ready to engage in discussions on economic and security issues.

item.title: CIA director travels to Havana to meet with Cuban officials

item.description: Republican mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt reposted an AI-generated video depicting him as a Batman-inspired character saving a burning Los Angeles from his opponents, raising questions about how AI is reshaping elections. NBC News’ Steve Patterson reports.

media:content.media:title: AI-generated video supporting Spencer Pratt for mayor of L.A. goes viral adding to concern over fake campaign ads
media:content.media:description: Republican mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt reposted an AI-generated video depicting him as a Batman-inspired character saving a burning Los Angeles from his opponents, raising questions about how AI is reshaping elections. NBC News’ Steve Patterson reports.

item.title: AI-generated video supporting Spencer Pratt for mayor of L.A. goes viral adding to concern over fake campaign ads

item.description: The Supreme Court ordered that patients could receive the abortion pill mifepristone through the mail and without an in-person appointment with a clinician after a ruling by the New Orleans-based 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals on May 1 had imperiled widespread access to the pill.

media:content.media:title: Supreme Court allows abortion pill to remain available by mail
media:content.media:description: The Supreme Court ordered that patients could receive the abortion pill mifepristone through the mail and without an in-person appointment with a clinician after a ruling by the New Orleans-based 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals on May 1 had imperiled widespread access to the pill.

item.title: Supreme Court allows abortion pill to remain available by mail

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Kansei Matsuzawa, 27, became the first Japanese-born player ever signed by an NFL team when he agreed to a deal as an undrafted free agent with the Raiders.

media:content.media:text: An illustration depicting Kansei Matsuzawa kicking a football on an orange background surrounded by stars
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

media:content.media:text: Kansei Matsuzawa prepares to kick a football
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

media:content.media:text: Kansei Matsuzawa in his Hawaii Rainbow Warriors uniform on the football field
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

media:content.media:text: Kansei Matsuzawa walks on stage with a large Japanese flag draped over his shoulder
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

item.title: YouTube taught a Japanese teen how to kick field goals. Now he’s in the NFL.

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts

item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Robinson
item.description: President Trump touts trade deals with China but did not share details.

media:content.media:text: CHINA-US-DIPLOMACY
media:content.media:description: US President Donald Trump (C) walks with China's President Xi Jinping (L) at Zhongnanhai Garden in Beijing on May 15, 2026.

media:content.media:text: FDA Commissioner Dr. Marty Makary
media:content.media:description: FDA Commissioner Dr. Marty Makary in August 2025.

media:content.media:text: Trump's Iran Deadline Looms With Mediators Pushing For Truce
media:content.media:description: John Ratcliffe, director of the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), during a news conference in the James S. Brady Press Briefing Room of the White House in Washington, DC, US, on Monday, April 6, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce sits on a bench on a porch next to Jason, she has her hand on his chin as he holds an orange toy
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares for her 28-year-old son, Jason, who has a rare chromosome disorder and requires constant supervision.

item.title: Trump wraps visit with Xi Jinping and CIA director meets with Cuban officials: Morning Rundown

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Kansei Matsuzawa, 27, became the first Japanese-born player ever signed by an NFL team when he agreed to a deal as an undrafted free agent with the Raiders.

media:content.media:text: An illustration depicting Kansei Matsuzawa kicking a football on an orange background surrounded by stars
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

media:content.media:text: Kansei Matsuzawa prepares to kick a football
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

media:content.media:text: Kansei Matsuzawa in his Hawaii Rainbow Warriors uniform on the football field
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

media:content.media:text: Kansei Matsuzawa walks on stage with a large Japanese flag draped over his shoulder
media:content.media:description: Kansei Matsuzawa, nicknamed "the Tokyo Toe" by a University of Hawaii teammate, is a kicker for the Las Vegas Raiders.

item.title: YouTube taught a Japanese teen how to kick field goals. Now he’s in the NFL.

item.dc:creator: Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: President Donald Trump’s Medicaid cuts and emerging attacks on paid family caregiving have people fearing financial ruin as they fight to keep loved ones out of institutions.

media:content.media:text: Melissa Gonce sits on a bench on a porch next to Jason, she has her hand on his chin as he holds an orange toy
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: Jason stands on a set of stairs inside of his home
media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

media:content.media:text: A four image composite: top left image shows a pill being poured out of a pill bottle onto a hand, top right shows Melissa helping Jason put on a shirt, bottom left show Melissa preparing food on a countertop, and bottom right shows Melissa shaving Jason's face

media:content.media:description: Melissa Gonce cares full time for her 28-year-old son, Jason, through a Medicaid program facing major cuts. She expects to lose $18,000 a year.

item.title: Families caring for disabled relatives face unthinkable choices as Medicaid cuts loom

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Berkeley Lovelace Jr.
item.description: As Democrats look for a viable healthcare message ahead of the midterm elections, one well-connected think tank is pushing the party to embrace a new idea: free primary care for all Americans

media:content.media:text: A supporter of the Affordable Care Act demonstrates in front of the Supreme Court on Nov. 10, 2020.
media:content.media:description: The proposed free primary care wouldn’t cover things like surgery, hospital stays, cancer treatment or most specialized care.

item.title: Free primary care for all: Democratic think tank pushes the party on new health policy

item.description: Rubio spoke about Iran, Taiwan, Cuba and more during President Donald Trump’s trip to Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.title: Read the full transcript: Secretary of State Marco Rubio interviewed by ‘NBC Nightly News’ anchor Tom Llamas

item.dc:creator: Angela Yang
item.description: Spencer Pratt’s bid for Los Angeles mayor gained viral traction after AI videos, which Pratt said were “fan-made,” circulated across social media.

media:content.media:text: Spencer Pratt seated on a white couch with his hands folded in his lap.
media:content.media:description: TV personality and Los Angeles mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt visits "Fox & Friends" at Fox News Channel Studios in New York, on Jan. 28, 2026.

item.title: AI-generated pro-Spencer Pratt mayoral campaign videos point to a new political reality

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: After a slow trickle of leaks and announcements, the NFL announced its schedule for the 2026 season Thursday. These are the best matchups.

media:content.media:text: Buffalo Bills v Kansas City Chiefs
media:content.media:description: Patrick Mahomes of the Kansas City Chiefs and Josh Allen of the Buffalo Bills after their game in Kansas City, Mo., in 2022.

media:content.media:text: Seattle Seahawks v Los Angeles Rams - NFL 2025
media:content.media:description: Patrick Mahomes of the Kansas City Chiefs and Josh Allen of the Buffalo Bills after their game in Kansas City, Mo., in 2022.

item.title: Chiefs-Bills, Patriots-Seahawks among top 10 games of the 2026 NFL season

item.dc:creator: Janis Mackey Frayer
Dawn Liu
Jay Ganglani
item.description: They’ve never met, but Ping Ping and Fu Shuang are getting ready for the blind date of a lifetime.

media:content.media:text: Ping Ping in Chengdu, China in May 2026.
media:content.media:description: Ping Ping in Chengdu, China in May 2026.

media:content.media:text: Fu Shuang in Chengdu, China, in early May.
media:content.media:description: Fu Shuang in Chengdu, China, in early May.

item.title: Pandas set for 8,000-mile blind date in Atlanta

item.dc:creator: Dan De Luce
Abigail Williams
Maya Rosenberg
Phil Helsel
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with Cuban officials in Havana, according to a CIA official and a statement from the Cuban government.

media:content.media:text: Trump's Iran Deadline Looms With Mediators Pushing For Truce
media:content.media:description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe in Washington last month.

item.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe meets with Cuban officials in Havana

item.dc:creator: Dareh Gregorian
Kyle Stewart
item.description: The House Ethics Committee announced Thursday it is investigating Rep.

media:content.media:text: Chuck Edwards
media:content.media:description: Edwards' office told NBC News in a statement that he welcomed any investigation.

item.title: House Ethics panel probes sexual harassment allegations against Chuck Edwards

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: Republicans hope to eliminate a majority-minority seat held by longtime Democratic Rep. Jim Clyburn before the November midterm elections.

media:content.media:text: South Carolina Governor
media:content.media:description: Republican South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster had initially decided not to call a special session.

item.title: South Carolina governor calls for a special session on redistricting

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: REI’s biggest sale of the year is a great time to find deals on big brands like REI Co-op, The North Face, Merrell, Black Diamond and more.

media:content.media:text: REI is one of the largest outdoor retailers nationwide, and this is its biggest sale of the year.
media:content.media:description: REI is one of the largest outdoor retailers nationwide, and this is its biggest sale of the year.

media:content.media:text: REI is one of the largest outdoor retailers nationwide, and this is its biggest sale of the year.
media:content.media:description: REI is one of the largest outdoor retailers nationwide, and this is its biggest sale of the year.

item.title: The 50+ best deals at REI’s Anniversary Sale

item.dc:creator: Bethany Heitman
item.description: We found the best affordable summer dresses for travel on Amazon — from casual minis to breezy maxi dresses, all under $50.

media:content.media:text: Amazon offers spring and summer dresses in a variety of silhouettes and styles.
media:content.media:description: Amazon offers spring and summer dresses in a variety of silhouettes and styles.

media:content.media:text: Amazon offers spring and summer dresses in a variety of silhouettes and styles.
media:content.media:description: Amazon offers spring and summer dresses in a variety of silhouettes and styles.

item.title: 11 packable spring dresses under $50 hiding on Amazon

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: I learned the best way to clean stainless steel appliances, including the expert-recommend tools to use, like microfiber cloths and stainless steel wipes.

media:content.media:text: It’s safe to use stainless steel wipes and sprays to clean your appliances without scratching them.
media:content.media:description: It’s safe to use stainless steel wipes and sprays to clean your appliances without scratching them.

item.title: How to clean stainless steel appliances in just 4 steps

item.dc:creator: Bethany Heitman
item.description: According to dermatologists, a lymphatic drainage facial involves massaging your face in upward motions to decrease puffiness and give you glowier skin.

media:content.media:text: To maximize results, experts say it’s important to massage your face in gentle, upward motions.
media:content.media:description: To maximize results, experts say it’s important to massage your face in gentle, upward motions.

media:content.media:text: To maximize results, experts say it’s important to massage your face in gentle, upward motions.
media:content.media:description: To maximize results, experts say it’s important to massage your face in gentle, upward motions.

item.title: What does a lymphatic drainage facial do and should you try it?

item.description: At least two people were killed when a small plane crashed into a house in Akron, Ohio. Authorities say no injuries were reported from on the ground at the crash site.

media:content.media:title: At least two people dead after small plane crashes into house in Ohio
media:content.media:description: At least two people were killed when a small plane crashed into a house in Akron, Ohio. Authorities say no injuries were reported from on the ground at the crash site.

item.title: At least two people dead after small plane crashes into house in Ohio
